Title: Auxin-salicylic acid seesaw regulates the age-dependent balance between plant growth and herbivore defense

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense against phloem-feeding insects; contributes to age-related resistance but excessive accumulation suppresses early growth
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: salicylic acid biosynthesis via the phenylalanine ammonia-lyase pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor flux for SA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, supporting PAL-derived salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expression increases with plant age; higher in 35-day-old adult plants than 21-day-old juvenile plants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher in adult leaves; repressed by auxin and promoted when auxin synthesis is inhibited
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: plant age; whitefly infestation; exogenous salicylic acid; exogenous IAA/NAA; auxin biosynthesis inhibitors L-kynurenine and Yucasin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS silencing; Agrobacterium-mediated overexpression; RT-qPCR; SA quantification; whitefly bioassays; promoter transactivation assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes SA accumulation, NbPR1 expression, and resistance to whiteflies; silencing reduces age-related resistance
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step supported in this study for age-dependent SA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native NbPAL6 promoter in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: elevated SA-dependent defense is associated with growth inhibition in juvenile plants at the system level
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: upregulation could enhance insect resistance in adult or production stages, but deployment should avoid excessive early SA that compromises growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among nine NbPAL genes examined, only NbPAL6 showed age-dependent induction linked to ARR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbPAL6 was the only tested PAL family member whose expression increased with age. 2. Silencing NbPAL6 in adult plants reduced SA, NbPR1 expression, and whitefly resistance. 3. Overexpressing NbPAL6 in juvenile plants increased SA accumulation and resistance, establishing NbPAL6 as a core SA-biosynthetic determinant of ARR.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense against whiteflies and age-related herbivore resistance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: salicylic acid biosynthesis via transcriptional activation of NbPAL6
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates NbPAL6 transcription by binding MYB cis-elements in the NbPAL6 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NbPAL6 promoter DNA; upstream activation by NbARF18La; NbARF18Lb
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: plant age; auxin negatively regulates its transcript abundance indirectly through NbARF18La/b and NbmiR160c
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key transcriptional activator connecting NbARF inputs to NbPAL6-driven SA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: higher in 35-day-old adult plants than in 21-day-old juvenile plants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: repressed by IAA; enhanced when auxin biosynthesis is inhibited
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: plant age; exogenous IAA; auxin biosynthesis inhibitors; whitefly defense context
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native NbMYB42 promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; nuclear localization; GUS and dual-luciferase assays; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; VIGS silencing; overexpression; SA quantification; whitefly bioassays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: activates NbPAL6, increases SA and NbPR1, and enhances whitefly resistance; silencing compromises adult resistance
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: activation of SA defense is part of a module antagonistic to juvenile growth through auxin-SA seesaw
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful regulator to enhance SA-mediated insect defense, but should be stage-specific or inducible to minimize growth penalty
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ChIP-qPCR showed enrichment at multiple promoter regions (P1, P3, P4, P5), and EMSA validated direct binding to the P3 element
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbMYB42 directly binds the NbPAL6 promoter and activates its transcription. 2. Overexpression in juvenile plants strongly elevates NbPAL6, SA, and whitefly resistance. 3. Silencing in adult plants decreases NbPAL6 expression, SA accumulation, and ARR.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against phloem-feeding insects, especially whiteflies
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ARF family; N-terminal B3 DNA-binding domain and C-terminal dimerization domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional control of SA biosynthesis through the NbMYB42-NbPAL6 module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates NbMYB42 transcription by binding the AuxRE site in its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: acts in an auxin-responsive feedforward module negatively gated by auxin-induced NbmiR160c
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms heteromer with NbARF18Lb and homomer/self-association of NbARF18La
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: auxin; NbmiR160c-mediated posttranscriptional repression; plant age
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: age-enhanced positive regulator upstream of NbMYB42 and NbPAL6 needed for adult SA accumulation and resistance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: higher in 35-day-old adult plants than 21-day-old juvenile plants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: reduced by IAA; increased by auxin biosynthesis inhibitors; not repressed at promoter activity level by auxin
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: plant age; IAA; NAA; Yucasin; L-kynurenine
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native NbMYB42 promoter and NbARF18La promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; nuclear localization; GUS and dual-luciferase assays; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; Y2H; BiFC; pull-down; VIGS silencing; overexpression; SA quantification; whitefly bioassays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes NbMYB42 and NbPAL6 expression, SA accumulation, and whitefly resistance; silencing reduces ARR
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: the pathway it activates is opposed by high auxin in juvenile plants and linked to growth-defense balance
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for enhancing herbivore resistance via SA, ideally combined with management of miR160/auxin effects or stage-specific expression
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: miRNA-seq and degradome sequencing identified/validated upstream NbmiR160c targeting of ARF transcripts; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Directly binds P2 AuxRE motif in NbMYB42 promoter; activation of NbPAL6 is indirect through NbMYB42
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbARF18La directly activates NbMYB42 by binding an AuxRE in its promoter. 2. It physically interacts with NbARF18Lb and self-associates. 3. Overexpression increases SA-linked defense, whereas silencing reduces SA and whitefly resistance.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against phloem-feeding insects, especially whiteflies
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ARF family; N-terminal B3 DNA-binding domain and C-terminal dimerization domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional control of SA biosynthesis through the NbMYB42-NbPAL6 module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates NbMYB42 transcription by binding the AuxRE site in its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: acts in an auxin-responsive feedforward module negatively gated by auxin-induced NbmiR160c
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: auxin; NbmiR160c-mediated posttranscriptional repression; plant age
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive regulator required for age-enhanced SA accumulation and whitefly defense
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: higher in 35-day-old adult plants than 21-day-old juvenile plants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: reduced by IAA; increased by auxin biosynthesis inhibitors; not repressed at promoter activity level by auxin
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: plant age; IAA; NAA; Yucasin; L-kynurenine
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native NbMYB42 promoter and NbARF18Lb promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; nuclear localization; GUS and dual-luciferase assays; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; Y2H; BiFC; pull-down; VIGS silencing; overexpression; SA quantification; whitefly bioassays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes NbMYB42 and NbPAL6 expression, SA accumulation, and whitefly resistance; silencing reduces ARR
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: part of auxin-SA growth-defense tradeoff network
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate positive regulator for engineering insect resistance, preferably with temporal control to reduce growth penalties
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: miRNA-seq and degradome sequencing identified/validated upstream NbmiR160c targeting of ARF transcripts; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Directly binds P2 AuxRE motif in NbMYB42 promoter; cooperates with NbARF18La
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbARF18Lb directly activates NbMYB42 transcription. 2. It cooperates with NbARF18La to induce NbPAL6 and SA accumulation. 3. Silencing impairs adult whitefly resistance, while overexpression enhances juvenile defense.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against whiteflies and modulation of growth-defense balance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: posttranscriptional repression of ARF-mediated activation of SA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: cleaves and represses NbARF18La/b transcripts, indirectly lowering NbMYB42 and NbPAL6 expression
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: auxin-induced negative arm of the auxin-SA seesaw
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major negative regulator that suppresses the NbARF-NbMYB42-NbPAL6 module and thereby lowers SA accumulation in juvenile plants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: higher in 21-day-old juvenile plants than in 35-day-old adult plants; induced by IAA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: IAA increases its expression and promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: plant age; IAA treatment; transient overexpression; target mimic inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native NbmiR160c promoter in GUS/LUC reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: miRNA-seq target prediction; overexpression; target mimicry inhibition; degradome sequencing; GFP/GUS/LUC sensor assays; RT-qPCR; SA quantification; whitefly bioassays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: represses SA accumulation and whitefly defense by silencing NbARF18La/b; inhibition of NbmiR160c enhances SA pathway and defense
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: relieves defense in juvenile plants to preserve auxin-associated growth; blocking it can strengthen defense but may shift growth-defense balance
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: a promising editing or target-mimic candidate for improving insect resistance, but likely requires spatial/stage control to avoid growth penalties
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: miRNA-seq and degradome sequencing used to identify and validate cleavage of NbARF18La/b; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Auxin promotes NbmiR160c transcription via its promoter; target sites lie in coding regions of NbARF18La and NbARF18Lb
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbmiR160c is induced by auxin and is more abundant in juvenile plants. 2. It directly cleaves NbARF18La and NbARF18Lb transcripts. 3. Overexpression lowers NbMYB42, NbPAL6, SA, and whitefly defense, whereas target mimicry produces the opposite effects.
